title: Action Economy and Turn Structure
description: Rules governing how actions work in sequence and the types of actions available

one_thing_at_a_time.principle: The game uses actions to govern how much you can do at one time. You can take only one action at a time.
one_thing_at_a_time.importance: This principle is most important in combat, but applies in all situations

examples.social_interaction: In a social interaction, you can try to Influence a creature or use the Search action to read the creature's body language, but you can't do both at the same time
examples.exploration: When you're exploring a dungeon, you can't simultaneously use the Search action to look for traps and use the Help action to aid another character who's trying to open a stuck door (with the Utilize action)

bonus_actions.description: Various class features, spells, and other abilities let you take an additional action on your turn called a Bonus Action

requirements.availability: You can take a Bonus Action only when a special ability, a spell, or another feature of the game states that you can do something as a Bonus Action
requirements.default_state: You otherwise don't have a Bonus Action to take

limitations.quantity: You can take only one Bonus Action on your turn, so you must choose which Bonus Action to use if you have more than one available
limitations.timing: You choose when to take a Bonus Action during your turn unless the Bonus Action's timing is specified
limitations.dependency: Anything that deprives you of your ability to take actions also prevents you from taking a Bonus Action

examples.description: Allows a Rogue to take a Bonus Action for specific abilities
Attack with off-hand weapon as a bonus action
Cast as a bonus action to heal at range

reactions.description: Certain special abilities, spells, and situations allow you to take a special action called a Reaction

characteristics.trigger_based: A Reaction is an instant response to a trigger of some kind, which can occur on your turn or on someone else's
characteristics.most_common: The Opportunity Attack is the most common type of Reaction

limitations.frequency: When you take a Reaction, you can't take another one until the start of your next turn
limitations.interruption: If the reaction interrupts another creature's turn, that creature can continue its turn right after the Reaction

timing.default: A Reaction takes place immediately after its trigger unless the Reaction's description says otherwise

common_reactions.name: Opportunity Attack
common_reactions.trigger: A creature leaves your reach without using the Disengage action
common_reactions.response: Make one melee attack against the creature

common_reactions.name: Counterspell
common_reactions.trigger: A creature within range casts a spell

common_reactions.name: Shield (spell)
common_reactions.trigger: You are hit by an attack or targeted by Magic Missile
common_reactions.response: Increase AC and potentially negate the attack

per_turn_limits.action: One action per turn (unless specifically granted additional actions)
per_turn_limits.bonus_action: One bonus action per turn (only if a feature grants it)
per_turn_limits.reaction: One reaction per round (resets at start of your next turn)
per_turn_limits.movement: Movement up to your Speed (can be broken up)
per_turn_limits.free_interactions: One free object interaction per turn

timing_flexibility.action: Taken during your turn at any point you choose
timing_flexibility.bonus_action: Taken during your turn at any point you choose (unless timing specified)
timing_flexibility.reaction: Taken immediately after trigger occurs (on any creature's turn)
timing_flexibility.movement: Can be broken up before and after your action
timing_flexibility.interaction: Must occur during movement or action

character_motivation.roleplay_question: Ask yourself as you play, 'What would my character do?'
character_motivation.approach: Playing a role involves some amount of getting into another person's head and understanding what motivates them and how those motivations translate into action
character_motivation.context: In D&D, those actions unfold against the backdrop of a fantastic world full of situations we can only imagine. How does your character react to those situations?
character_motivation.caveat: Avoid character choices that ruin the fun of the other players and the DM. Choose actions that delight you and your friends.
